audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Ananias/Sapphira, awe, signs, healing, arrest, angelic release, council, Gamaliel, rejoicing under dishonor.

verses.restored: But a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession.
He kept back from the price, his wife also knowing it, and bringing a certain part, laid it at the apostles' feet.
Peter said, Ananias, why has the adversarial impulse filled your heart to lie to the holy Spirit and keep back from the price of the field?
While remaining, did it not remain yours? And after being sold, was it not in your authority? Why did you set this deed in your heart? You did not lie to humans but to the Divine.
Hearing these words, Ananias fell down and breathed out; great awe came upon all who heard.
The younger ones rose, wrapped him, carried him out, and buried him.
It happened about three hours later that his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
Peter answered her, Tell me whether you sold the field for so much. She said, Yes, for so much.
Peter said to her, Why was it agreed by you to test the Spirit of the Lord? Look, the feet of those who buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.
Immediately she fell at his feet and breathed out. The young ones came in and found her dead, and carrying her out, buried her beside her husband.
Great awe came upon the whole assembly and upon all who heard these things.
Through the hands of the apostles many signs and wonders were happening among the people, and they were all with one accord in Solomon's Portico.
None of the rest dared join themselves to them, yet the people magnified them.
More than ever, those trusting in the Lord were added, multitudes of both men and women.
So they even carried the weak into the streets and placed them on beds and mats, so that as Peter came, even his shadow might overshadow some of them.
The multitude also from the cities around Jerusalem came together, carrying the weak and those troubled by unclean spirits, and they were all being healed.
But the high priest rose up, and all those with him, the party of the Sadducees, and they were filled with jealousy.
They laid hands on the apostles and put them in public custody.
But during the night a messenger of the Lord opened the doors of the prison, brought them out, and said,
Go, stand in the temple and speak to the people all the words of this life.

Hearing this, they entered the temple at daybreak and were teaching. The high priest came, and those with him, and called together the council and all the elders of the children of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
But when the officers arrived, they did not find them in the prison, and returning, reported,
saying, We found the prison locked with all security and the guards standing at the doors, but opening, we found no one inside.
When the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these words, they were perplexed about them, what this might become.
Someone came and reported to them, Look, the men whom you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
Then the captain went with the officers and brought them, not with force, for they feared the people, lest they be stoned.
Bringing them, they stood them in the council, and the high priest questioned them,
saying, We strictly commanded you not to teach in this name, and look, you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and intend to bring this human's blood upon us.
Peter and the apostles answered, It is necessary to obey the Divine rather than humans.
The Divine of our fathers raised Jesus, whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
This one the Divine exalted at his right hand as Leader and Savior/Healer, to give turning/realignment to Israel and release of sins/misalignments.
We are witnesses of these words, and so is the holy Spirit whom the Divine gave to those obeying him.
Hearing this, they were cut through and were intending to kill them.
But a certain Pharisee in the council, Gamaliel by name, a teacher of the Law honored by all the people, rose and commanded that the humans be put outside for a little while.
He said to them, Men of Israel, take care for yourselves concerning these humans, what you are about to do.

For before these days Theudas rose, saying he himself was someone, to whom a number of men, about four hundred, attached themselves; he was killed, and all who were persuaded by him were dissolved and came to nothing.
After this Judas the Galilean rose up in the days of the registration and drew away people after him; that one also perished, and all who were persuaded by him were scattered.
And now I say to you, stand away from these humans and release them; because if this counsel or work is from humans, it will be overthrown.
But if it is from the Divine, you will not be able to overthrow them, lest you even be found fighting against the Divine.
They were persuaded by him, and calling the apostles, they beat them, commanded them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and released them.
Therefore they went from the face of the council rejoicing that they were considered worthy to be dishonored for the Name.
Every day, in the temple and house by house, they did not cease teaching and announcing the good news: Jesus the Messiah.
